text:
$AAOI looks a bit extended but no denying the strength both in the photonics basket as well as this name specifically. 200 should come in short order if this holds up.

Two ways to play it: retest of the 10d SMA for the lower-risk entry, or a breakout through 160.

Watch LITE, GLW and the rest of the photonics basket for confirmation, always best to see names within a group/theme move together.

text: @aleabitoreddit $VGO.WA Vigo Photonics is my favourite long on photonics sector.

Yes, that's the photonics chain powering next-gen AI interconnects. Sivers' DFB lasers pair with POET's Optical Interposer for scalable light engines in CPO and pluggable transceivers. POET enables Celestial AI's Photonic Fabric platform, which Marvell acquired to supercharge optical scale-up for their AI chips and data centers. Clear tailwind for $SIVE in this ecosystem.

text:
Frontrunning 1.6T/CPO within the broader photonics supercycle is the most compelling investment to me. 

I have high conviction in that statement.

Which is why I'm long the entire supply chain (+1 extra bottlenecK)

1. $SIVE - Their laser revenue scales aggressively with $JBL, $MRVL, Ayar, O-Net.

And I do think CPO/1.6T will blow away any conservative analyst projections from how hard $NVDA, $GOOGL, and others have been pushing photonics architectures.

Downside risk is multi-sourcing, but there's a reason Jabil chose Sivers. When you compare $MTSI, $LITE, $COHR, Furukawa, and others.

There's genuinely not many laser suppliers in the entire world... they're all $10B+, then you have this mini CHIPS act chokepoint trading at <$1B MC.

2. Shunsin (6451) - I don't see how it's possible Foxconn's optical foundry for testing, packaging, and assembly is valued at $1.5B MC less than $LWLG. 

When they look extremely derisked piggybacking off of Foxconn's photonics volume.

$TSM's optical arm VisEra example is ~$5B, but they scale H2 2028 from Gen-3. Foxconn looks to be ramping up just next year.

They're just scaling low fwd p/e multiples off of $NVDA CPO supply chain demand in Taiwan and all public indicators point to capacity expansion + extreme demand. 
 
3. Win Semi - They're the foundry for Sivers to scale up DFB laser production. As well as $AVGO, SpaceX supply chains and others.

When I do supply chain mapping and Win Semi pops up in every single frontier supply chain I see. 

There's probably something markets are not pricing in.

4. $MRVL - I find this genuinely compelling as a mini-Broadcomm. 

Their potential design with with $GOOGL today, helps the case past 2028.

But the catalyst I was looking at was $MSFT Maia ramp, which happens H2 2026, and likely keep scaling up exponentially into 2027, 2028, 2029. 

Celestial acquisition was probably the smartest thing in the world for them. Maybe on next drop or CSP? 

5. $HPS.A - Transformers/Switchgears are commodities  + boring parts of the DC supply chain.

However, when the bottleneck is 2-5 years, and you have backlog increasing 100%+... causing extreme shortages.

It's only up 20%+ since my thesis post, but I do see this being de-risked given massive backlog visibility (even though it's inferred, they don't give exact #). 

I do think markets are missing something, especially with potential gross margin expansion from price hikes if they pull it off.... 

Again backlog + demand just de-risks this company,  and it seems like a high growth compounder post facility expansion last year. 

There's many others like $NBIS, $JBL, $RPI, $TSEM, $LITE, $ARM, $SOI, $AXTI,  $IQE, $ALRIB, Fittech, PCL, and others that I'm very fond of, but just mentioning 5 off the top of my head from today's prices... if I'm creating a new portfolio. 

Of course, it's good to barbell with other uncorrelated companies to AI supply chains, but these are just 5 I liked.

"Frontrunning 1.6T/CPO within the broader photonics supercycle is the most compelling investment to me. I have high conviction in that statement. Which is why I'm long the entire supply chain (+1 extra bottlenecK) 1. $SIVE - Their laser revenue scales aggressively with $JBL, $MRVL, Ayar, O-Net. And I do think CPO/1.6T will blow away any conservative analyst projections from how hard $NVDA, $GOOGL, and others have been pushing photonics architectures. Downside risk is multi-sourcing, but there's a reason Jabil chose Sivers. When you compare $MTSI, $LITE, $COHR, Furukawa, and others. There's genuinely not many laser suppliers in the entire world... they're all $10B+, then you have this mini CHIPS act chokepoint trading at <$1B MC. 2. Shunsin (6451) - I don't see how it's possible Foxconn's optical foundry for testing, packaging, and assembly is valued at $1.5B MC less than $LWLG. When they look extremely derisked piggybacking off of Foxconn's photonics volume. $TSM's optical arm VisEra example is ~$5B, but they scale H2 2028 from Gen-3. Foxconn looks to be ramping up just next year. They're just scaling low fwd p/e multiples off of $NVDA CPO supply chain demand in Taiwan and all public indicators point to capacity expansion + extreme demand. 3. Win Semi - They're the foundry for Sivers to scale up DFB laser production. As well as $AVGO, SpaceX supply chains and others. When I do supply chain mapping and Win Semi pops up in every single frontier supply chain I see. There's probably something markets are not pricing in. 4. $MRVL - I find this genuinely compelling as a mini-Broadcomm. Their potential design with with $GOOGL today, helps the case past 2028. But the catalyst I was looking at was $MSFT Maia ramp, which happens H2 2026, and likely keep scaling up exponentially into 2027, 2028, 2029. Celestial acquisition was probably the smartest thing in the world for them. Maybe on next drop or CSP? 5. $HPS.A - Transformers/Switchgears are commodities + boring parts of the DC supply chain. However, when the bottleneck is 2-5 years, and you have backlog increasing 100%+... causing extreme shortages. It's only up 20%+ since my thesis post, but I do see this being de-risked given massive backlog visibility (even though it's inferred, they don't give exact #). I do think markets are missing something, especially with potential gross margin expansion from price hikes if they pull it off.... Again backlog + demand just de-risks this company, and it seems like a high growth compounder post facility expansion last year. There's many others like $NBIS, $JBL, $RPI, $TSEM, $LITE, $ARM, $SOI, $AXTI, $IQE, $ALRIB, Fittech, PCL, and others that I'm very fond of, but just mentioning 5 off the top of my head from today's prices... if I'm creating a new portfolio. Of course, it's good to barbell with other uncorrelated companies to AI supply chains, but these are just 5 I liked."}}}, "quoted_status_result": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "core": As AI clusters scale past copper’s physical limits, the bottleneck shifts to optical & these are the companies building that layer across the stack: 

1. $AAOI building the transceiver layer of the AI network through vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. It has already secured over $200M in its first volume 1.6T order from one hyperscale customer followed by another $124M in 800G orders from a second.

2. $AEHR building the reliability layer for the optical & AI hardware stack through burn-in & test systems. It just received a record $41M follow-on order from its lead hyperscale customer reinforcing the idea that Sonoma is becoming a key production burn-in platform for high-power AI ASICs.

3. $CRDO building the connectivity layer that helps AI clusters move data faster through active electrical cables, retimers & high-speed interconnect silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition also extends that platform into silicon photonics before copper becomes a real constraint.

4. $LITE building the laser layer of the AI optical stack through EMLs, optical components & optical switching exposure. The setup is backed by a $2B $NVDA strategic investment & optical circuit switch backlog above $400M with orders reportedly extending through 2028.

5. $VIAV building the testing & validation layer of the optical stack through network instrumentation & photonics measurement tools. It is the picks-and-shovels layer of the transition because every high-speed optical buildout still needs to be tested regardless of which transceiver vendor wins.

6. $COHR building one of the core photonics bottlenecks through indium phosphide lasers, optical engines & communications components tied to next-gen AI networking. It also has a $2B $NVDA strategic investment behind it & is doubling InP device capacity into the 1.6T ramp.

7. $MRVL building the DSP & optical infrastructure layer through electro-optics, PAM DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom networking chips. The Celestial AI deal & NVLink Fusion exposure both strengthen its position as photonics becomes more central to AI cluster design."}}}, "rest_id": "2045885512922419251", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "304051", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2046003571603423359", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "10827", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
This map shows why Sivers - ( $SIVE ) is quietly becoming one of the most important names in AI photonics.

Sivers sits in the center of the CPO ecosystem supplying the critical multi-wavelength laser arrays to:

•  Jabil (1.6T LRO pluggables)
•  Ayar Labs (optical I/O)
•  O-Net + Enablence (external light source modules)
•  POET (hybrid integration, now tied to Marvell + Google TPU discussions)

That’s direct or indirect exposure to $META , $AMZN , $MSFT , $GOOGL , and $MRVL — basically the entire hyperscaler map.

Still only ~$720M market cap

The Nasdaq listing is coming. The commercial ramps are coming.

text:
The Copper Wall Account 🧱➡️💡

My thesis: The next wave of AI infrastructure is the shift from copper interconnects to silicon photonics and Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) in data centers. Power efficiency and bandwidth demands are forcing the change and I’m adding exposure to quantum computing.

Here’s my initial DCA plan:

Starting tomorrow: $5/week into each of these 10 stocks 

$TSM $AVGO $MRVL $LITE $AAOI $CRDO $FN $TSEM $IONQ $HON

•  Photonics/AI infrastructure play: TSM, AVGO, MRVL, LITE, AAOI, CRDO, FN, TSEM

•  Quantum long-shot: IONQ + HON (via Quantinuum)

I’ll post weekly updates on performance, news, and any adjustments.

If the AI buildout accelerates and silicon photonics/CPO gets widely adopted, I expect strong returns by 2030.

If IONQ or Quantinuum leads in quantum, this 10-year DCA could compound beautifully by 2040.

Who’s in on the next tech wave? Drop your thoughts 👇

text:
This is the most comprehensive photonics stack there is. 

L1: Materials & Substrates

The foundation. Without this, nothing above it exists.
L1 companies grow the raw crystal wafers – InP, GaAs, Ge that silicon can’t replace for high-speed photonics.

Key names: $AXTI, $SOI, $IQE, $AIXA, $COHR, $LASR

Top performer: AXT (AXTI) +6,837%

InP demand exploded for AI datacenter optical connectivity. Q3 2025 InP revenues up 250%+ sequentially. Doubling capacity by end of 2026. 

L2: Active Photonic Devices

This is where light gets made.
Lasers, modulators, photodetectors. In a CPO world, these get embedded directly into switch ASICs – no longer pluggable add-ons. That’s a structural demand shift, not a cycle.

Key names: $LITE, $COHR, $LASR, $SIVEF, $AAOI, $MTSI, $SMTC

Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685%

Named NVIDIA collaborator on Spectrum-X silicon photonics. Recently received the largest single CPO laser purchase commitment in company history.

L3: Electro-Optical Connectivity ICs

The translators. Silicon that bridges optical and electrical worlds.

TIAs, drivers, DSPs, SerDes, retimers. As speeds push to 800G and 1.6T, this layer becomes a chokepoint.

Key names: $MTSI, $SMTC, $MRVL, $CRDO, $ALAB, $MXLV

Top performer: Credo (CRDO) +361%

Not a pure photonics play – but every 800G and 1.6T deployment runs through their AEC and SerDes IP.

L4: PIC Platform & IP

The brain of the photonic system.
Waveguides, modulators, and photodetectors on a single chip. Highest-IP layer in the stack. Whoever locks platform relationships here wins the next decade.

Key names: $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO, $IOLI, $EOPT (Ayar Labs, Lightmatte)

Top performer: Innolight +948%

Partnered with Tower Semi on a breakthrough SiPh platform that cuts lasers per module in half.

L5: Foundry & Process Platform

You need a fab. Not just any fab.
SiPh fabrication needs specialized processes – TFLN, SiPh COUPE, SoIC – that only a handful of foundries can run globally.

Key names: $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, $TSM

Top performer: Tower Semi (TSEM) +542%

SiPh revenue hit ~$52M in Q3 2025 alone, up 70% YoY. Full-year 2025 target: $220M+. Q4 annualized run rate: $320M+. Investing $650M to triple SiPh shipments by mid-2026.

L6A: Engine & Module

Where the optical engine becomes a product – 400G, 800G, 1.6T modules.
Most commoditized layer today. CPO transition will redraw it entirely.

Key names: $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $FN, $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO

Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685%

Vertically integrated across L2 and L6A. Supply locked by hyperscalers. Pricing power persists.

L6B: Infrastructure

Fiber and cable. The physical nervous system of every AI cluster.
Boring until hyperscalers start spending $50B+ on datacenter campuses. Then it’s anything but.

Key names: $GLW, $OTCPK:SMOKY, $PRY, $APH, $FJKUF

Top performer: Fujikura (5803.TSE) +748%

Japanese fiber giant. Direct beneficiary of AI intra-datacenter cabling demand going parabolic.

L6C: Transport

Moves data between datacenters across metro and long-haul fiber.
As AI clusters get larger and more distributed, inter-datacenter bandwidth demand compounds. This layer doesn’t slow down.

Key names: $CIEN, $NOK, $CSCO

Top performer: Ciena (CIEN) +777%

WaveLogic coherent optics is the dominant long-haul platform in North America and Europe

L7: Test & Qualification

The final gatekeeper. Every photonic device passes through here before shipping.

Burn-in, optical test, IC test, wafer test. Often overlooked. Often the highest-margin layer.

Key names: $KEYS, $TER, $ATE, $FRT, $AEHR, $VIAV, $COHU

Top performer: Aehr Test (AEHR) +954%

Shipped the world’s first production wafer-level burn-in system for AI processors. Now diversified into silicon photonics, GaN, and hard disk drives.

Great job on putting it together  @damnang2

$SIVE

I just found out about this.

I only analyzed the photonics part.

Are you telling me that my largest position combines photonics (the booming sector) and space (the next booming sector)?

Come on.

I’m not selling anything until a 10x at the very least.

But few talk about this:
They lost a SATCOM RFQ to MixComm… then acquired them a year later.

Now they sit across: 
• AI compute (photonics) 
• SATCOM/Wireless (defense, satellite, 5G/6G, DoD) 

Two mega trends. One story.

text:
As of April 23, 2026

The AI Photonics and Optical Infrastructure sector is showing a sharply bifurcated tape today, with large-cap AI infrastructure names holding green while speculative small and micro-cap photonics names unwind hard, the top loser being $IQE down 20.53%. The move reflects a parabolic blow-off on the London line (IQE.L ran over 107% in the two weeks into April 17 before rolling over), with the OTC mirror IQEPF gapping down today on profit-taking and zero fresh fundamental news into the May 20 earnings print (https://t.co/udYbhT79LN).

The quality leadership is sending a clear signal: capital is rotating out of speculative substrate and early-stage PIC names into the picks-and-shovels plays with visible AI capex pull-through. $MRVL leads at +5.24% on the Amazon-Anthropic $100B deal tailwind layered on top of the Google custom AI chip talks and Nvidia's $2B NVLink Fusion investment, which together anchor Marvell as the default optical DSP and custom ASIC partner across every hyperscaler (https://t.co/2jwCYbswdK).

$MRVL: 5.24%
$VIAV: 4.15%
$TER: 4.10%
$CIEN: 3.38%
$KEYS: 2.23%
$GFS: 1.90%
$FORM: 1.50%
$GLW: 0.44%
$BESIY: -0.21%
$SPX: -0.41%
$NDX: -0.57%
$VICR: -1.84%
$AEHR: -1.90%
$ATEYY: -2.01%
$TSEM: -2.34%
$LITE: -3.06%
$COHR: -3.65%
$SLOIF: -6.97%
$SIVEF: -7.78%
$AAOI: -7.82%
$POET: -8.22%
$LWLG: -10.29%
$AXTI: -13.42%
$XNDU: -16.98%
$IQE: -20.53%

1. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking
$CIEN, $GLW: The large-cap optical layer held up on AI fabric demand, with Ciena catching a bid as 800G and 1.6T buildouts continue into 2H26 and Corning benefiting from the optical cable pull-through.
$LITE, $COHR: Both names gave back modestly after a multi-week run, looking like position-trimming rather than thesis breakdown ahead of earnings season.
$AAOI: Down 7.82%, this is the sharpest move among the transceiver names and reads as profit-taking on the recent domestic-manufacturing tariff-exemption bid plus microcap beta to the broader sector unwind.

2. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries
$GFS, $TSEM: Foundries split, with GlobalFoundries up on SiPho and advanced node traction while Tower gave back some of its recent run on sector risk-off.
$AXTI, $IQE, $SLOIF, $SIVEF: This is where the damage concentrated. AXTI down 13.42%, IQE down 20.53%, Soitec down 6.97%, SiTime down 7.78%. InP substrate demand remains the bottleneck thesis intact, but the OTC liquidity and parabolic-unwind dynamics are doing the driving today, not any fundamental deterioration.
$BESIY: Flat on the day, advanced packaging and hybrid bonding exposure providing some ballast.

3. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
$POET, $LWLG, $XNDU: All three got hit hard (POET -8.22%, LWLG -10.29%, XNDU -16.98%) in what looks like a coordinated speculative unwind. Cramer's measured POET comments on CNBC this morning didn't help sentiment on the name, and the broader early-stage PIC basket tends to move as one when risk comes off. Thesis remains long-dated optionality, but the tape is punishing anything that ran too far too fast.

4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure
$TER, $KEYS, $FORM, $VIAV: The test and measurement complex was the cleanest trade on the board today, with Teradyne, Keysight, FormFactor, and Viavi all green on the read-through from strong AI infrastructure capex guidance. This is where the sector's earnings visibility is most concrete.
$MRVL, $ATEYY: Marvell up 5.24% as the clear AI infrastructure beneficiary, while Advantest gave back a bit on what appears to be profit-taking after a strong run.
$AEHR, $VICR: Both down modestly, with AEHR's move tracking the broader SiC and compound semi test cycle concern rather than company-specific news.

The AI Photonics and Optical Infrastructure complex went risk-on across the board, led by a 10%+ rip in $SLOIF after Bloomberg crowned Soitec the best-performing European stock of 2026, up more than 300% YTD with an 84% gain this month alone , and Oddo BHF raised its target to €85 from €50 (https://t.co/EbZP37PIaz). SPX +1.20% and NDX +1.29% gave the tape a clean risk-on backdrop.
Photonics has moved from optionality to consensus theme. Hyperscaler capex is flowing through transceivers into PICs and the upstream substrate layer, and the market is now hunting picks-and-shovels exposure with structural moats. Today's laggards are mostly OTC liquidity friction or single-name overhang, not thesis breaks.

1. Compound Semi Substrates & Foundries
SLOIF, IQE, AXTI, SIVEF: The substrate layer is where the photonics money is being priced. SLOIF parabolic on Bloomberg coverage and the Oddo target hike. IQE down 11% on an insider sale headline, with Andrew Nelson dumping roughly £1.5M of stock at £0.49 per share, the biggest IQE insider sale in a year Simply Wall St, classic profit-taking on the YTD leader. AXTI continues to act as the cleaner US-listed InP proxy. SIVEF whipped on OTC liquidity, not fundamentals.
GFS, TSEM: Foundry leverage to the photonics buildout. TSEM is the silicon photonics workhorse for Tower's CPO ramp, GFS riding the broader AI custom silicon wave.

2. Optical Transceivers, DSP Silicon & Active Networking
COHR, CIEN, AAOI, LITE, MRVL: 800G/1.6T transceiver demand still the cleanest hyperscaler read-through. COHR leading today, MRVL strong on merchant DSP silicon read-through, AAOI holding bid on the 1.6T order book, LITE quiet after the Nvidia investment digestion phase.
GLW, VIAV: GLW slightly red on profit-taking after its run. VIAV ripping +7% as network test demand benefits from the same buildout.

3. Equipment, Test & Packaging
FORM, AEHR, TER, KEYS: Wafer-level test and burn-in picks-and-shovels trade. FORM and AEHR both up sharp on continued read-through from HBM and AI accelerator volume. TER and KEYS round out the test exposure.
ATEYY, BESIY: ATEYY (Aixtron) is the MOCVD tool play for compound semi growth, shares more than doubled in 2026. BESIY is the hybrid bonding leverage to advanced packaging and CPO.

4. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
LWLG: Modest green on EO polymer optionality, still pre-revenue, sentiment-driven.
POET, XNDU: Both red. POET digesting after recent rally. XNDU off 7.5% on profit-taking after a parabolic run on Nvidia's Ising quantum AI catalyst, trading at extreme price-to-sales on roughly $4.6M revenue and a $70.7M net loss Invezz, classic mean-reversion in the speculative bucket."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2044554804744360306"}}, "rest_id": "2045355011358802237", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "31281", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2047447518049649062", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "17439", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

user_name: Asteris - Socials on Autopilot with Your Content!
text:
Photonic computing for edge AI is one of those stories that sounds niche until you think about energy.

If NeuraWave delivers faster inference with lower power draw, that matters anywhere AI has to live outside giant data centres.

#EdgeAI #Photonics #AIHardware

Anders Storm’s repeated, specific praise is a strong and credible counter to blanket “pumper” accusations against Serenity (@aleabitoreddit). @stormdirac 

Storm is not a random retail account: He was CEO of Sivers for 8–10 years (built the photonics side). He knows the tech, partnerships (Ayar Labs, Jabil, etc.), and execution realities better than almost anyone outside the current management. 

He’s not hyping blindly - he engages with Serenity’s threads on supply-chain mapping and calls them “great deep research.”

This isn’t random. Photonics is becoming a key bottleneck in AI infrastructure: faster data transfer, lower power, and critical for next-gen data centers.

If compute was the first wave, photonics could be next.

Price action is starting to reflect it.

$LITE $COHR $AXTI $AAOI 

#stocks #AI #photonics #semiconductors #investing

text:
Marvell $MRVL Announced they will be acquiring Polariton Technologies 

They plan to add high speed low power plasmonics based silicon photonics devices to its portfolio

This gives Marvell a stronger presence in optical technology and positions them better for next gen connectivity

text:
$poet and $sivef indirect benefits to this $mrvl acquisition-

$poet Technologies (POET) sees indirect positive momentum, not a direct tie to Polariton itself.POET’s core tech is its Optical Interposer platform—a wafer-scale, passive-alignment hybrid photonics solution that integrates light sources, modulators, waveguides, etc., into compact optical engines/light engines. It’s upstream in the same AI interconnect value chain Marvell is aggressively building out.
Marvell’s prior Celestial AI acquisition (Dec 2025) already made POET a beneficiary: Celestial used POET’s tech (e.g., Blazar light sources) as a key enabler for its Photonic Fabric. Marvell inherited that supply relationship, and POET has confirmed purchase orders/shipments tied to it.

This new Polariton deal further validates Marvell’s heavy commitment to scaling photonics for AI (now adding plasmonic modulation on top of SiPh). It doesn’t displace POET—it reinforces the ecosystem demand for integrated optical engines. Analysts and investors have repeatedly called out POET as a “real winner” or potential M&A target in the wake of Marvell’s optics moves, as hyperscalers/NVIDIA/Marvell need proven, scalable integration platforms. 

$POET gets another leg of validation and potential order acceleration. It’s not a direct “Polariton synergy” play, but the Marvell optics flywheel helps POET’s positioning in the same AI connectivity wave.

 $sivef is a strong indirect beneficiary and one of the more credible pure-plays in the exact layer this deal targets.Sivers Photonics specializes in high-performance DFB laser arrays, CW (continuous-wave) lasers, and external light source modules explicitly designed for AI data-center optical interconnects, CPO, pluggables, and SiPh integration.
They have established partnerships in the ecosystem (e.g., with POET for light engines, Ayar Labs, and paths into Marvell/Celestial/NVIDIA optics roadmaps via the CW-WDM MSA and related collaborations). Their lasers are a critical “fuel” for SiPh-based modulators and engines—like the plasmonic + SiPh solutions Marvell just acquired.
This deal accelerates adoption of exactly the architectures Sivers supplies (1.6T+ LRO transceivers, CPO, ultra-low-power AI interconnects). Recent Sivers news (e.g., Jabil collaboration on 1.6T modules) already shows momentum; Marvell’s move pours more fuel on the fire for laser array demand.

text:
$GFS GlobalFoundries 

定位:AI 数据中心 → 光互联 → 硅光(Silicon Photonics)代工厂

坐标系:
光引擎里的两种主流技术路线:一种基于磷化铟(InP,做激光最好,也是 Sivers / AXT 的主场),另一种基于硅(硅光 / SiPh,做调制和波导最适合 CMOS 量产)。

硅光的思路是"把光学器件用成熟半导体工艺做在硅晶圆上",好处是能借用半导体行业几十年积累的产能、良率、成本曲线。问题是硅本身不会发光,所以硅光要和 InP 激光配合使用 —— 这是 POET 这类"封装平台"存在的原因。

硅光需要专门的工艺和专门的晶圆厂。GlobalFoundries 就是这个赛道的最大代工厂。

公司背景:
美国 Malta, NY 总部,全球纯代工厂第三名(TSMC、Samsung 之后,和 UMC、SMIC 同级)。不做最先进制程(3nm/5nm 是 TSMC 的地盘),专做 12–28nm 的特殊工艺节点 —— 射频、模拟混合信号、功率管理、SOI、以及硅光。

2025 年 11 月收购了新加坡 AMF(Advanced Micro Foundry),这是全球第一家商业化硅光纯代工厂,拥有 200mm 硅光平台。

加上之前收购的埃及 InfiniLink(光学数据连接芯片创业公司),GlobalFoundries 宣称自己是全球最大的纯代工硅光晶圆厂。

技术路线:
纽约工厂做 300mm 硅光,新加坡(AMF)做 200mm 硅光,两地互补。

GF 同时在做 InP 激光和硅 CMOS 的单片异构集成,长期目标是做出"一块晶圆上同时有激光、调制器、驱动、波导"的完全集成光引擎。

赛道位置:
硅光代工这个环节格局是:Intel 做自用的(自己的 CPO 产品)、TSMC 给 Broadcom 做、GlobalFoundries 是最大的纯代工(向所有人开放)。

Broadcom、Marvell、Cisco、Coherent、Lumentum 这些公司的硅光芯片,相当一部分会在 GF 的晶圆厂生产。

它的地位相当于硅光赛道的"TSMC" —— 不做自己品牌产品,做所有人的代工。

财务和公司状态:
市值约 200 亿美金,2026 年硅光业务预计贡献 AMF 收购后 +7500 万美金,目标 2030 年前硅光年收入突破 10 亿美金。

硅光市场本身以 25% CAGR 增长。

主体业务(非硅光)还是传统代工,受汽车和工业需求拖累,财务表现平淡。2026 年 3 月做了二次公开发行 + 并行股份回购,市场反应一般。

所以 GFS 的股价走势更像"被硅光故事带动的大盘股",不是纯粹硅光标的。

AI 周期里的角色:
"硅光代工"的稀缺标的。

想直接投硅光,美股里几乎没有纯正选项(Ayar Labs 未上市,Lightmatter 未上市),GFS 是公开市场最直接的硅光赋能者 —— 但要意识到它的非硅光业务占比还很大,硅光故事被稀释。

核心赌注:
硅光在 2027–2030 年真正从 CPO 需求起量;
GF 在开放代工市场能拿住大部分份额;
主体代工业务不继续恶化。

仓位定位:
硅光主题的代理持仓(proxy play),不是纯正选项。

和 POET / Sivers 形成互补:POET / Sivers 是"如果硅光/CPO 跑通 10 倍股"的押注,GFS 是"硅光跑通它至少有稳定订单"的底仓。

适合追求硅光敞口但又不想承担 early-stage 波动的配置。

关键观察点:
AMF 整合进度和新加坡硅光产能爬坡 → 
Broadcom / Marvell 等大客户的硅光订单走向 → 
主体代工业务汽车/工业板块能否止跌。

硅光的卖铲人。

text: TXN showing signs that Analog Chip Season may finally be taking off. I’ve been talking about this coming transition in the hardware trade since Q4 of last year and it finally seems like it may be upon us. The first screenshot below hurts my soul, me pumping Photonics in early December but not longing any of them, but that’s the macro tourist game for ya. Regardless, this signals the continuing evolution of the manufacturing boom going on globally. In a time where energy constraints are picking up, any hardware that can bypass those constraints will get bid up here big time, and that is exactly what Analog chips provide, energy efficiency. Listen I know I’ve been banging the drum on potential trouble coming to the AI Datacenter Buildout but I don’t think it is going to break yet or crash the economy yet. As long as NVDA remains elevated, even if flat, capital from that sector will continue to slosh around into different ancillary corners of the same trade. I.E. as NVDA has languished since Q3 of last year, memory stocks have been on a tear. I think we are going to see now a rotation from this existing capital base start going into the Analog semiconductor names. I think as manufacturing output begins to diversify away from AI, I think having exposure to Analog Semis will help buttress any pain from the AI Hardware trade that should come, once demand destruction starts getting actualized. I think I’m going to finally get some exposure to them as I think it is a great asymmetric hedge to my Downstream AI thesis being expressed thru META RDDT & IBM calls.

text: Marvell $MRVL is going strong into Photonics 🧐 Looks to me they want to control as much as possible of the supply chain (remem. my thesis about the Marvell - $POET deal, that perhaps it's only interested in the Interposer System, NOT in the POET Transceivers ==> no $SIVE Lasers)
embedded_text:
Marvell $MRVL just announced it has agreed to acquire Polariton Technologies, 

"a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices"

Terms of the deal were not disclosed

$COHR - Coherent
$POET - POET Technologies 
$LITE - Lumentum Holdings 
$IPGP - IPG Photonics

text:
The AI infrastructure bottleneck nobody talks about.

As AI clusters scale past copper's physical limits, optical photonics becomes the critical layer.

These companies are building the infrastructure layer that powers the next generation of AI systems—and the investment signals that come with them.

A massive data throughput upgrade is coming. The question is which companies capture the value.

text:
I've been quietly watching on the sideline for a couple of months now.

Everybody and their grandmas are hyping photonics (and for a very good reason, no doubt on that!) but there are truly a few people doing the actual work and a ton of parrots.

A handful of persons truly do the research and others just copy/paste it, reformulate it, ask AI to generate something they didn't even read and then post.

text:
Spot on by @Chaamacoo. 🎯

People keep trying to put $SIVE / $SIVEF in a single box. It’s not just an "AI stock", a "defense stock", or a "telecom stock." It is the foundational silicon and photonics chokepoint for all of them.

Think of them as the "Intel Inside" of the next hardware supercycle. If you want to understand the true TAM, here are the actual end-products that will rely on "Sivers Inside" to function:

🧠 AI Datacenters: Next-gen 1.6T LRO pluggable transceivers (Jabil/Marvell) & Co-Packaged Optics.

 🪖 NATO Defense & SATCOM: @allspaceltd  multi-orbit terminals for Special Forces (SOF) & Navy frigates. 

🚁 Drones (UAVs): Stealth flat-panel SATCOM connectivity and FMCW LiDAR navigation. 

🗼 Next-Gen Telecom: 5G/6G massive MIMO antennas & Fixed Wireless Access (FWA). 

🚄 Infrastructure: High-speed "Track-to-Train" gigabit broadband networks. 

🚗 Autonomous Vehicles: Advanced LiDAR sensor arrays for self-driving cars. 

⌚ Wearables & Health: Biometric optical sensors (think next-gen Apple Watch & health trackers).

You aren't buying a single theme hoping it plays out. You are buying the underlying crossroads of tomorrow's technology. The sum-of-the-parts (SOTP) valuation could be huge. ⏳🔥
$SIVE $SIVEF #Semiconductors #Photonics

"richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2439}, {"from_index": 2465, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2499}]}, "text": "What makes a company truly compelling is not just what it is today, but how many powerful trends it could end up riding at once. That is a big part of why $SIVE / $SIVEF stands out to me. Most investors build exposure one theme at a time. They buy one company for AI, another for defense, another for satcom, another for photonics, and maybe yet another for next-generation telecom. It is a bit like assembling the future one puzzle piece at a time, hoping the picture eventually comes together. Sivers feels different. It feels more like finding one piece that already connects several of the most important parts of that picture. At first glance, it is easy to label it as just another semiconductor company. But the more I look at it, the more that feels like an oversimplification. Sivers sits in the overlap between mmWave, photonics, datacenter connectivity, satcom and defense. Those are not niche or isolated markets. They are major long-term themes, and the company has exposure to several of them at once. That is what makes the setup so compelling. Sivers does not need every single door to open at the same time for the story to work. It may be enough for one or two of these markets to accelerate, one or two partnerships to deepen, or one part of the business to gain meaningful commercial traction before investors begin to see the company in a very different light. That is why I think the “one stock, several themes” angle matters here. For a new investor, Sivers may be easier to understand if you stop thinking about it as a single narrow bet and start thinking about it as a small platform with multiple shots on goal. Not one straight road, but a crossroads. Not one engine, but several potential drivers of value that could begin contributing at different times. Of course, that does not remove the risk. Stories with several moving parts are never simple. But they can also create exactly the kind of asymmetry that makes small-cap investing so interesting, especially when the market has not fully decided how to categorize the company yet. And that is often where the upside starts to emerge. So the appeal of Sivers, to me, is not just that it has exposure to attractive end markets. It is that you do not need to buy five different companies to get that exposure. You are looking at one company that could potentially benefit from several major technology shifts at once. That kind of setup is rare. And that is why I think Sivers deserves a much closer look."}}}, "rest_id": "2045914937076183188", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "10502", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2045961814413115759", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "7039", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Photonics is a stack! 
$AXTI $AIXA $TSEM $AEHR $LITE $GLW

Here is a clean map of the sector.
Please bookmark & share! 

A laser, a photonic chip, a finished module, and a deployed AI network all sit at different points in one long chain. 

Different companies get paid for totally different reasons and on totally different timelines.

I just updated my layer framework so you can instantly see where every major name fits that I track (and why the AI buildout lifts the whole stack).

Quick map:

Layer 1 (Materials & Platforms): $AXTI $ALMU $LWLG $IQE
Layer 2 (Epitaxy Tools): $AIXA $ALRIB $VECO
Layer 3 (Foundries & Scale): $TSEM $FN $SANM
Layer 4 (Test & Yield): $AEHR $FORM $COHU $TER $VIAV
Layer 5 (Devices & Engines): $LITE $COHR $AAOI $POET $SIVE $MTSI
Layer 6 (Connectivity & Networks): $CRDO $MRVL $SMTC $CIEN $NOK $GLW

There are many more names in the industry that didn't make this list. 

Which are you tracking the closest?

text:
Agreed—the signals are aligning faster than consensus expects. Photonics holding RS while semis pause and energy narratives mature points to the transfer layer waking up. 

Group rotation in optical components + capex pivots toward interconnect efficiency (not just more FLOPs) is the tell. COHR’s positioning in that bandwidth chokepoint looks timely.

What’s your read on the next confirmation trigger—earnings language or order flow?

@grok It’s already happening @grok Semis are crowded.
Energy is understood. Now you’re seeing:

-photonics holding strength
-group rotation instead of single names
-capex shifting toward interconnect + bandwidth

That’s the early phase. Not obvious yet…but it’s forming.

text:
Remember, I lived next door to a confirmed robotics and photonics expert, Mr. Pierrick Vulliez. This should not be difficult to understand—even for Grok.

In the footage, the hand movements of the man standing in the trench align point-for-point with the robot digger’s shovel movements. This is one of several videos I’ve posted over the past few days suggesting how closely a human operator must be controlling the machine, and all involve precise hand or head movements by the controller from what I’ve seen.

If the trench activity were human controlling another human that would imply a level of coordination I’m not prepared to interpret beyond what my current evidence supports.

$LASE — Valye Company Analysis
Laser Photonics Corporation’s latest quarterly filing reveals expanding top-line efforts driven by strategic acquisitions, notably in pharmaceutical laser applications through Control Micro Systems. Despite substantial net losses and liquid
Key points:
• Q4 2025 results show widened net losses but progressing revenue from expanded product portfolio.
• Acquisition of Control Micro Systems broadens addressable market into recession-resistant pharmaceutical manufacturing.
• Vertically integrated production supports cost control, protects proprietary technology, and enhances pricing flexibility.
• Laser blasting disrupts traditional abrasive methods across automotive, aerospace, healthcare, shipbuilding, and nuclear sectors.
• Government contracts include innovative defense projects like the Laser Shield Anti-Drone System (LSAD).

text:
I'm confused how Jenoptik, $JEN, is up 65% YTD?

The "Photonics" label is doing a lot of heavy lifting for some pretty shaky fundamentals:

-> $JEN is a Tier 2 supplier, but getting priced like a monopoly. High capex + R&D exposure without pricing power of true lithography giants like $ASML

-> ​€100M capex on their new fab is just going to kill margins. With the semi-recovery looking lumpy in Q2 2026, the fixed cost drag could lead to a huge earnings miss by end of yr.
​
-> ​26x P/E for a business that is still 30% low-margin traffic safety and automotive metrology. The "pure photonics" rebrand is a marketing ploy, and not a financial or operational reality yet.

-> In the Photonics segment, Chinese competitors are eroding the moat in micro-optics and laser modules. $JEN is being squeezed between the high-end (Zeiss) and the low-cost (MLOptic).

$JEN is just a cyclical hardware firm w/ significant non-core baggage being priced like a secular AI winner.

embedded_text:
Everyone talks about $SIVE and AI photonics.

But few talk about this:
They lost a SATCOM RFQ to MixComm… then acquired them a year later.

Now they sit across: 
• AI compute (photonics) 
• SATCOM/Wireless (defense, satellite, 5G/6G, DoD) 

Two mega trends. One story.

The recent strong rally in Credo Technology (CRDO) was sparked by its April 14 announcement acquiring DustPhotonics for ~$1.2B (cash + stock + earnouts). This adds silicon photonics tech for 800G/1.6T+ optical transceivers, expanding beyond active electrical cables into faster AI data center interconnects—targeting $500M+ optical revenue by FY27.

It built on Jefferies' fresh Buy rating, explosive AI-driven growth (200%+ YoY revenue), and bullish order flow signals. Stock up ~46% in the past 5 days as of April 23.

@Thomas_james_1 $LITE near $850 vs $SIVE at $3 — valuation divergence in photonics: fundamentals or market cap effects? Small-cap photonics names offer higher beta in upcycles but with higher volatility — option spreads may work better than outright shares.

text:
Leeway AI: STM pivots to data centers while automotive stalls and politics cloud the room
$STM

STMicroelectronics started mass production of silicon photonics chips for AI data centers in Q1 2026 and expanded its 800-volt DC power portfolio alongside NVIDIA, while deepening ties with AWS to strengthen its position in AI infrastructure.

The company completed its acquisition of NXP's MEMS business, expected to contribute mid-range revenue in the $40 million area over time, with particular strength in automotive and industrial sensors.

In China, STM began series production of its STM32 H7 microcontroller family, with plans to extend to H5 and C5 variants, improving supply chain resilience against geopolitical pressure.The momentum came against headwinds in Q4 2025. A net loss of $30 million, impairment charges and restructuring costs totaling $141 million, and a 31.6% revenue decline in the power and discrete segment weighed heavily on results.

Management is budgeting $2 to $2.2 billion in capital expenditure for 2026, mostly directed toward SiC and 300-millimeter capacity expansion.The company sees normalization of customer inventory levels by the end of Q2 2026 as the critical threshold for sustainable recovery.

text:
THE OPTICAL PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK...🧐

​As AI clusters blast past the physical limits of copper interconnects, the bottleneck is shifting hard into high-speed optics.

​These are the key players building the essential layers of the AI optical stack:

$AAOI - Transceiver Layer

Dominating with vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. Already landed a major 1.6T order ($200M+) from a leading hyperscaler, plus strong 800G follow-on business.

$CRDO - Connectivity Layer

Powering high-speed links via AECs and advanced silicon. 
The DustPhotonics acquisition brings silicon photonics in-house, a massive edge as copper constraints intensify.

$LITE - Laser & Switching

Leader in EMLs and optical circuit switching. 
Backed by a $2B strategic investment from $NVDA with an optical switch backlog stretching into 2028.

$COHR - InP Infrastructure

Addressing the critical Indium Phosphide bottleneck. 
Holds a $2B $NVDA commitment and is scaling capacity aggressively for 1.6T+ networking.

$CIEN - Transport Layer

The backbone that ties everything together. 
Their solutions enable hyperscalers to move enormous AI traffic between data centers as clusters scale to gigawatt levels.

$FN - Manufacturing Backbone

The "picks-and-shovels" partner. As the reliable assembly giant for optical modules, they fuel the entire ramp where speed and scale determine the winners.

$MRVL - DSP & Electro-Optics

Strengthening the infrastructure with PAM DSPs and custom chips. 
The Celestial AI deal and NVLink exposure place them at the heart of next-gen cluster design.

$AEHR - Testing & Reliability

Providing essential wafer-level burn-in (FOX-XP). 
Recently secured a major leader in networking for 1.6T production ramp (critical for hyperscale reliability).

The transition from copper is no longer optional 😬
Anyway these stocks are volatile, with risks around customer concentration, production ramps (InP/SiPho), and high valuations. DYOR. NFA.

high-speed optics. ​These are the key players building the essential layers of the AI optical stack: $AAOI - Transceiver Layer Dominating with vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. Already landed a major 1.6T order ($200M+) from a leading hyperscaler, plus strong 800G follow-on business. $CRDO - Connectivity Layer Powering high-speed links via AECs and advanced silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition brings silicon photonics in-house, a massive edge as copper constraints intensify. $LITE - Laser & Switching Leader in EMLs and optical circuit switching. Backed by a $2B strategic investment from $NVDA with an optical switch backlog stretching into 2028. $COHR - InP Infrastructure Addressing the critical Indium Phosphide bottleneck. Holds a $2B $NVDA commitment and is scaling capacity aggressively for 1.6T+ networking. $CIEN - Transport Layer The backbone that ties everything together. Their solutions enable hyperscalers to move enormous AI traffic between data centers as clusters scale to gigawatt levels. $FN - Manufacturing Backbone The "picks-and-shovels" partner. As the reliable assembly giant for optical modules, they fuel the entire ramp where speed and scale determine the winners. $MRVL - DSP & Electro-Optics Strengthening the infrastructure with PAM DSPs and custom chips. The Celestial AI deal and NVLink exposure place them at the heart of next-gen cluster design. $AEHR - Testing & Reliability Providing essential wafer-level burn-in (FOX-XP). Recently secured a major leader in networking for 1.6T production ramp (critical for hyperscale reliability). The transition from copper is no longer optional 😬 Anyway these stocks are volatile, with risks around customer concentration, production ramps (InP/SiPho), and high valuations. DYOR. text:
"The deal adds plasmonics-based silicon photonics tech that could enable faster & more power-efficient optical interconnects for next-gen AI networking."

This looks very promising. but I don't think people know what the weak point is.

text:
$MRVL just made a move in the AI infrastructure race, acquiring Polariton Technologies to lock down the shift to 3.2T optical connectivity. 

​Why this is a big deal:
• ​Adds ultra-fast, low-power 
  plasmonics-based silicon photonics 
  to their stack.
• ​Solves the massive bandwidth and 
   power bottlenecks in next-gen AI 
   data centers.
• ​Positions Marvell perfectly as 
  architectures evolve beyond the 
  current 1.6T standard.

​Data centers need pure speed without melting the grid. Marvell is buying the exact tech to make it happen. 

$MRVL up 1.5% AH.

text:
Interesting take on the interconnect bottleneck. Photonics is indeed the physics play for scaling beyond copper limits—lower latency, power per bit, and heat as racks densify for AI training/inference. 

Data movement at exascale is the next constraint after raw FLOPs. COHR's optical tech sits right in that transfer layer. Capital cycles rotate to the real chokepoints. What's your edge on timing the shift?

Because copper-based systems hit limits:

-Heat
-Signal loss
-Power inefficiency
 
Photonics solves that. It allows data centers to scale without melting down. That’s where $COHR sits.
Right inside the transfer layer.

This is physics. You can’t scale AI if data can’t move efficiently.

text:
As models scale, something breaks:

-Bandwidth
-Latency
-Power per bit

That’s the real constraint forming. 
This is where photonics matters.

Light moves data faster
-More efficiently
-With less heat

That’s the shift most people haven’t priced in yet:
electrical → optical

Neo-clouds cooking with hot grease recently, but look a bit over extended. Nebius smartly consolidating. 

CPU's and broader semi packaging names look unbelievably strong, but a bit extended here.

Space trade is hot, but needs some sideways action.

text:
With a background in atomic precision measurements, I'm more optics, quantum sensing, and photonics "focused" in my current role, obviously for other applications, but thought if there was an intersection somewhere, some of the signatures of the Chronoflux could be probed with the sensors under development.

I haven't worked on Casimir devices yet, but they are on my radar. I am interested in phononic photonic resonators, especially in relation to tests of Orch OR, as well as PIC frequency combs for other sensing applications. So there's definitely some overlap.

Would be nice if you could implement the equations function in this document 🙏

The two Substack articles profile Nynomic AG as a German photonics holding company with LayTec as its key subsidiary, a quasi-monopolist in in-situ metrology for MOCVD reactors and etch processes powering AI data center lasers (InP) and power electronics (GaN). They highlight restructuring savings lifting 2026 margins to 6-8%, vertical integration across the value chain, and massive TAM expansion from optical transceivers and co-packaged optics. My analysis sees strong moats from proprietary databases, OEM lock-ins with Aixtron (90%+ share), and recurring software upsells despite past guidance misses and cyclical risks. Growth potential is substantial at 3-5x for LayTec revenue by 2028 driven by AI buildout, with Nynomic re-rating to peer multiples implying 2-3x stock upside. This stems from validated photonics bottlenecks via NVIDIA investments and staggered order cycles already showing Q4 2025 inflection.

text:
$GFS GlobalFoundries Inc. (GFS) Q4 FY2025 Post-Earnings Debrief

https://t.co/vZ2dYs7a7E

Thesis: GlobalFoundries delivered a Q4 FY2025 print that pushes the underwriting debate away from a mature-node recovery and toward whether silicon photonics, optical-module and advanced-packaging capabilities, CID growth, and geographically resilient specialty capacity can support a structurally higher-growth and higher-margin model. The strongest proof that this shift is already happening is that 2025 was the first full year with more than 60% of revenue outside smart mobile devices and automotive plus CID reached a record one-third of revenue, while elevated FY2026 capex and lower FCF conversion remain the central execution risk.

-Q1 2026 revenue, gross margin, and diluted EPS versus the $1.625B / 27% / $0.35 guide framework
-Investor Day detail on the silicon photonics bridge, including pluggables versus CPO timing, module-yield milestones, and the 40% gross-margin path
-Whether silicon photonics remains overwhelmingly pluggables-driven in 2026 while keeping the end-2028 $1B run-rate target credible
-Whether Malta optical-module flow, advanced packaging, and 200mm/300mm dual-region capacity support faster revenue conversion without yield disruption
-Whether the structural mix shift, now above 60% non-mobile revenue and one-third auto plus CID, keeps widening in 2026
-Whether government-support offsets, customer prepayments, and corridor-level debottlenecking keep FY2026 net capex and FCF conversion within the guided algorithm

(GFS) Q4 FY2025 Post-Earnings Debrief https://t.co/vZ2dYs7a7E Thesis: GlobalFoundries delivered a Q4 FY2025 print that pushes the underwriting debate away from a mature-node recovery and toward whether silicon photonics, optical-module and advanced-packaging capabilities, CID growth, and geographically resilient specialty capacity can support a structurally higher-growth and higher-margin model. The strongest proof that this shift is already happening is that 2025 was the first full year with more than 60% of revenue outside smart mobile devices and automotive plus CID reached a record one-third of revenue, while elevated FY2026 capex and lower FCF conversion remain the central execution risk. -Q1 2026 revenue, gross margin, and diluted EPS versus the $1.625B / 27% / $0.35 guide framework -Investor Day detail on the silicon photonics bridge, including pluggables versus CPO timing, module-yield milestones, and the 40% gross-margin path -Whether silicon photonics remains overwhelmingly pluggables-driven in 2026 while keeping the end-2028 $1B run-rate target credible -Whether Malta optical-module flow, advanced packaging, and 200mm/300mm dual-region capacity support faster revenue conversion without yield disruption -Whether the structural mix shift, now above 60% non-mobile revenue and one-third auto plus CID, keeps widening in 2026 -Whether government-support offsets, customer prepayments, and corridor-level debottlenecking keep FY2026 net capex and FCF conversion within the guided algorithm"}}}, "rest_id": "2045928117240541286", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "13322", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Looking to elevate your laser system performance? The Crystech RTP Q-Switch offers fast, reliable electro-optic modulation designed for demanding applications in photonics and laser engineering.
Built with RTP (Rubidium Titanyl Phosphate) crystal technology, this Q-switch delivers:
🔹 High damage threshold
🔹 Fast switching speeds
🔹 Excellent thermal stability
🔹 No piezoelectric ringing
Explore specs and availability:

text:
On $CRDO's acquisition of Dust Photonics.

The $TSEM foundry thesis is gaining traction, and it makes sense given Tower's SiPh capabilities.

Worth adding: $ALAB did the same thing last October, acquiring aiXscale Photonics. A copper-first interconnect company buying a small photonics startup to get into optics. Both companies made the same call.

I haven't seen anyone put these two side by side yet.

https://t.co/6ZZhAm3XsA
embedded_text:
Excellent episode. Don't feel bad if you didn't know about Dust Photonics pre-deal. They said $TSEM was probably the fab for Dust, so if $CRDO blows out the product, it is incremental demand for TSEM. Also, an interesting liquid cooling angle.

As it comes to CPU, my @openclaw

Everyone talks about $SIVE and AI photonics.

But few talk about this:
They lost a SATCOM RFQ to MixComm… then acquired them a year later.

Now they sit across: 
• AI compute (photonics) 
• SATCOM/Wireless (defense, satellite, 5G/6G, DoD) 

Two mega trends. One story.
New post to educate about SATCOM ⤵️

text:
源杰科技（https://t.co/ZBjZH3oFsS）是激光芯片IDM厂商，2013年1月由张欣刚（ZHANG XINGANG，美国籍，1970年生）在陕西咸阳创立。

他清华本科、南加州大学材料科学背景，曾任Luminent研发经理、Source Photonics研发总监。现任董事长兼总经理。

妹妹张欣颖任董事（持股约3.43%）。核心技术副总潘彦廷等。

主营2.5G-50G磷化铟激光芯片，用于数据中心、5G、FTTH等。2022年科创板上市，近期AI算力需求推高股价超茅台成A股新股王。官网：https://t.co/ekEJYAQGbk

The photonics move has been sharp. A lot of names already up big.

Still watching $LITE, $AAOI and $SMTC for cleaner entries. Rather buy consolidation than chase strength.
The AI networking theme isn't done. Just waiting for the right setup.

Anyone else still in this space? https://t.co/CrtRZYLS0I

text:
Optical communication is rapidly evolving, enabling faster and more efficient global data transmission. Innovations in photonics, fibre optics, and wavelength multiplexing are driving unprecedented bandwidth and reliability. Engineers at Hamamatsu Photonics are advancing high-sensitivity photodiodes and semiconductor laser technologies that underpin modern telecom and data centre infrastructure. These breakthroughs reduce signal loss, increase speed, and support terabit-scale networks across submarine cables and cloud systems. 

As global demand for real-time data grows, optical systems are redefining connectivity with higher efficiency and lower energy use. 

Learn more: https://t.co/osOJovJNha

#engineering #technology #optics #photonics 

@HamamatsuPhoton

text:
The Call for Presentations is still open for the 2026 OCP APAC Summit, taking place August 11 to 12 in Taiwan.

From AI infrastructure and advanced packaging to cooling, photonics, and open data centers, this is where the industry comes together to share what’s next.

This year’s program spans 11 key areas, including AI Clusters, Networking, Photonics, Chiplets & Advanced Packaging, and more.

Submit your proposal by May 18 and be part of the conversations shaping the future of open compute.

Learn more: https://t.co/lihu2mnu9k Registration is now open.

Thank you to our Selection Committee, which includes leaders from across the ecosystem who will guide speaker and content selection.

@OpenComputePrj
#OCPAPAC26 #OCP

text:
i keep getting $AXTI vibes from it.

What does $POET tech do?
Photonics is nextgen.  $AXTI proves this.  Placing the laser is labor intensive/expensive/time consuming manual process.  $POET integrates photonics into a chip module.

WHY BUY?
1) needed for next gen AI hardware.
$AMZN - AI chips hitting ceiling - $POET solves this
$MSFT - CHIP production wall - $POET solves this
$META - favors Open Compute standards.  Arista is leader in this and works w/ $POET.  $META + $POET partnership inevitable

$TSLA - investing in DataCenter + Chips.  Will need $POET to get to next gen status

$AVGO & $NVDA - logical that one will buy $POET to prevent competitors from having nextgen bottleneck.

2) low fear dilution - cash runway 8 years

3) moat - 60 patents - years for competitor to replicate

4) no competitor

Tidbit
$AXTI got 4 straight days of gap ups.  UNICORN RARE.  $POET got 3 straight days.

text:
$OPTX (Syntec Optics)
Photonics and optics are red hot right now, and this name is riding that wave. Syntec makes precision optical components like lenses, mirrors, and assemblies for semiconductors, medical devices, defense, and lasers. part of the leading sector theme, just broke out of a base and successfully retested the breakout level. 
Verdict: Buy for a short-term scalp or swing trade.

> next day stock shoots up 20%

PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK As AI clusters scale past copper’s physical limits, the bottleneck shifts to optical & these are the companies building that layer across the stack: 1. $AAOI building the transceiver layer of the AI network through vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. It has already secured over $200M in its first volume 1.6T order from one hyperscale customer followed by another $124M in 800G orders from a second. 2. $AEHR building the reliability layer for the optical & AI hardware stack through burn-in & test systems. It just received a record $41M follow-on order from its lead hyperscale customer reinforcing the idea that Sonoma is becoming a key production burn-in platform for high-power AI ASICs. 3. $CRDO building the connectivity layer that helps AI clusters move data faster through active electrical cables, retimers & high-speed interconnect silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition also extends that platform into silicon photonics before copper becomes a real constraint. 4. $LITE building the laser layer of the AI optical stack through EMLs, optical components & optical switching exposure. The setup is backed by a $2B $NVDA strategic investment & optical circuit switch backlog above $400M with orders reportedly extending through 2028. 5. $VIAV building the testing & validation layer of the optical stack through network instrumentation & photonics measurement tools. It is the picks-and-shovels layer of the transition because every high-speed optical buildout still needs to be tested regardless of which transceiver vendor wins. 6. $COHR building one of the core photonics bottlenecks through indium phosphide lasers, optical engines & communications components tied to next-gen AI networking. It also has a $2B $NVDA strategic investment behind it & is doubling InP device capacity into the 1.6T ramp. 7. $MRVL building the DSP & optical infrastructure layer through electro-optics, PAM DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom networking chips. The Celestial AI deal & NVLink Fusion exposure both strengthen its position as photonics becomes more central to AI cluster design."}}}, "rest_id": "2045885512922419251", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "304051", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2047051196981223802", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "152107", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Marvell $MRVL just announced it has agreed to acquire Polariton Technologies, 

"a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices"

Terms of the deal were not disclosed

$MRVL ... Marvell to acquire Polariton Technologies to advance next-gen silicon photonics and optical interconnects; financial terms not disclosed (157.32+6.01)
MRVL announced the acquisition of Polariton Technologies, a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices. The acquisition strengthens Marvell's optical technology portfolio by adding advanced modulation capabilities that enable continued scaling in bandwidth, power efficiency, and integration for next-generation coherent and optical interconnect platforms.

The holy trinity of speculative photonics is having itself a day! 

I have been a $LWLG man myself this cycle, $POET is doing bad things to Wolfe Research and here we are with $ALMU starting to stir.  These are doing way better than the "big boys" lately. https://t.co/EyCevE1I3y

$POET fine
$ALMU aside the massive bid / ask spread making it impossible for TRADrs -- does things like this... naughty list. https://t.co/57lkSNq4up

"richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "La prochaine microcaps Française qui va péter serait Memscap ? >La boîte a 39 M$ de Capitalisation boursière >Elle est directement en rapport avec la photonique, elle fabrique des atténuateurs Optiques Variables (VOA) MEMSCAP, à la base, c’est surtout un fournisseur de capteurs de pression de très haute précision pour l’aéronautique et le médical. Mais ils ont aussi un segment communications optiques. Ils fournissent des VOA basés sur technologie MEMS, utilisés pour gérer dynamiquement la puissance des signaux dans les réseaux fibre optique (datacenters, télécoms, amplis EDFA). Sur ce segment de niche, MEMSCAP est tout simplement leader mondial des puces MEMS VOA, avec une part de marché estimée à plus de 80% sur un marché d’environ 5 M$ au niveau puces, et 50 M$ au niveau produits finis. Pas l'ASML de la photonique mais une boite qui pourrait se rerater rapidement. D'ailleurs hier il y a eu un afflux plutôt anormal, ce qui me fait potentiellement penser à un positionnement d’un bon gros player. Si les microcaps continuent d'exploser, je parie que Memscap en bénéficiera probablement."}}}, "rest_id": "2045528106413199710", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "345389", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2045914495139099056", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "771", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Here's a quick summary of the 7 stocks building the optical photonics layer for AI clusters (as copper limits hit):

1. **$AAOI** – Transceivers & U.S. InP lasers (already $324M+ orders)
2. **$AEHR** – Burn-in/test systems for high-power AI chips ($41M record order)
3. **$CRDO** – High-speed cables, retimers & silicon photonics
4. **$LITE** – Lasers, components & optical switches ($2B NVDA backing)
5. **$VIAV** – Testing & measurement tools (picks-and-shovels play)
6. **$COHR** – InP lasers & optical engines ($2B NVDA backing)
7. **$MRVL** – DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom AI networking chips

These target the optical bottleneck as AI scales. (Logos match the post image.)

text:
$GFS — GlobalFoundries: The Only “China-Free” Silicon Photonics Foundry at Scale
Everyone needs a fab. GlobalFoundries acquired Singapore’s Advanced Micro Foundry (AMF) — arguably the most important pure-play silicon photonics fab outside the U.S. — giving GlobalFoundries a claim to being the largest dedicated SiPh foundry in the world. Its “GF Fotonix” platform has become the go-to for fabless photonics companies, and the AMF acquisition allows it to offer a China-free supply chain for optical chips — a selling point that may become mandatory for U.S. hyperscalers.  In a world of supply chain paranoia, this is a structural moat hiding in plain sight.

$MKSI — MKS Instruments: The Lab Equipment That Makes Photonics Manufacturing Possible
Nobody builds photonics fabs without MKS. MKS spotlighted new photonics solutions for AI, quantum, and biophotonics applications at Photonics West 2026, drawing fresh analyst upgrades.  With projected earnings growth of 24% in 2026, MKSI has the potential to continue its rally.  Laser power meters, beam delivery, optical process control — the unsexy tools that make every photonics fab run. If the fab count doubles, MKSI equipment demand doubles with it.

text:
$CRDO — Credo Semiconductor: Just Bought Its Way Into Silicon Photonics With a $750M Power Move
Already on the list as a small cap — but this news changes the thesis entirely. Credo agreed to acquire DustPhotonics for $750M, bringing industry-leading Silicon Photonics PIC technology in-house and expanding Credo’s addressable market across 800G, 1.6T, and 3.2T optical transceivers — positioning it with a vertically integrated connectivity stack spanning SerDes, DSP, Silicon Photonics, and system integration.  Management targets combined optical revenue above $500M by fiscal 2027.  Credo went from a DSP play to a full photonics stack company in one deal. Re-rate incoming.

text:
$IQEPF — IQE PLC: The UK Epitaxial Wafer Supplier That 10x’d in Four Months
IQE PLC, a struggling UK semiconductor firm with a market value of less than $70M at the start of 2026, soared more than 10 times in just four months  as photonics demand re-rated the entire substrate layer. IQE is one of Lumentum’s key suppliers for Indium Phosphide (InP) — the material used to make high-performance data center lasers that silicon cannot generate on its own.  Highest volatility on this list. Trades OTC in the U.S. as $IQEPF. If you want the rawest exposure to the InP chokepoint, this is it — with all the execution risk that comes with a company undergoing a strategic review.

$COHR — Nvidia’s Chosen Partner. The Laser Foundry of AI.
Coherent and Nvidia are pioneering next-generation silicon photonics to enable AI infrastructure at unprecedented scale, speed, and energy efficiency  — direct quote from Jensen Huang. Traditional optical component makers like Coherent have pivoted from transceiver assembly to becoming laser foundries — critical component manufacturers rather than finished goods producers. A structurally better position.

text: $GLW — Corning: The Fiber Glass Monopoly That Runs Under Every Data Center. Corning is the dominant fiber glass supplier — what the photonics supply chain calls a necessary materials company. None of this works without the fiber infrastructure holding it together.  AI traffic means more fiber everywhere. Corning doesn’t compete in photonics — it underlies it. Durable, defensible, boring in the best way.

"$VIAV: A Crucial Role In The AI Optical Supply Chain Let’s start with a thought You can fab all the lasers, build all the transceivers, lay all the fiber, and rack all the switches, but none of it goes live until someone proves the entire network actually works under real AI workloads. That last-mile validation layer is where $VIAV sits. And as clusters get denser and speeds jump from 800G → 1.6T → 3.2T, this is becoming a more crucial role The question we need to answer is whether or not the whole fabric behaves, scales, and stays resilient under live AI traffic. $VIAV owns the tools that sit right after the optical hardware and right before full network confidence like: lab/production test platforms ultra-dense fiber certification gear system-level validation. Key products already hitting hyperscalers: 1) TestCenter D2 1.6T Appliance - generates real AI workloads and validates performance, scale, and multi-vendor interoperability at 1.6T. Management says current validation tech is “struggling to keep pace with AI.” 2) DCX 700 optical loss test set - certifies up to 24 fibers at once in dense data-center plants. Data-center demand has jumped to about a third of field instruments. 3) Hollow-core fiber tester - already in trials with three leading hyperscalers for campus-scale AI interconnects. Lower latency, longer reach. Management says fiber value chain “firing at full speed,” with hyperscalers demanding tighter control over every strand. Numbers are catching up: - Q2 revenue $369M (+36% y/y) - NSE (the AI/data-center engine) +46% and now 79% of total. - Business mix flipped: only ~40% traditional telco, 60% data-center + A&D. Q3 guide shows momentum continuing through calendar 2026. New Spirent assets tracking ~$200M run rate. Restructuring is ongoing, cutting slow segments to double down on data center and defense. Keep this one on your radar!
